step_by_step_thinking:
Your Response here:

I agree with agent1’s recommendation. In this case, nocturnal enuresis without any signs of urinary tract infection, abnormal lab findings, structural anomalies, or developmental delay typically resolves by school age due to maturation of bladder control mechanisms. Therefore, initial management should be conservative with parental education and follow-up in six months.
